headline: Chief Secretary to Government serves as both regulator and regulated
context: The Chief Secretary to the Government simultaneously chairs the public service reform committee and leads the civil service being reformed, creating an inherent conflict of interest.

cards.big: Chief Secretary chairs reform committee for the civil service he also leads
cards.sub: Person being reformed is leading their own reformation — classic conflict of interest

cards.big: No legislation prevents the Chief Secretary from chairing reform oversight bodies
cards.sub: Structural conflict exists because governance architecture was never designed for this scenario

cards.big: Civil service reform programme has RM240 million budget with limited external evaluation
cards.sub: Internal leadership of reform means internal assessment of reform outcomes

cards.big: Singapore's Public Service Division reports to PM directly, separate from service head
cards.sub: Structural separation between reform driver and service leadership prevents conflict

cards.big: Asking the civil service to reform itself is like asking students to grade their own exams
Should civil service reform be led by an independent body outside the service itself?
